name: web-games
description: Web browser game development principles. Framework selection, WebGPU, optimization, PWA.
capabilities: Select appropriate game framework based on project requirements (Phaser, PixiJS, Three.js, Babylon.js)
Implement WebGPU with WebGL fallback for cross-browser compatibility
Optimize game performance using asset compression, lazy loading, and object pooling
Configure PWA features for offline play and home screen installation
Handle browser audio constraints with proper user interaction requirements
Apply browser-specific best practices for mobile and desktop games
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 6 potential issues including external_commands, weak crypto, and system reconnaissance. Manual review confirms all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is pure markdown documentation providing browser game development guidance. Line 19/32 show ASCII decision trees (not shell backticks), lines 3/155 are description text (not crypto), and lines 30/80 are game dev terms (not reconnaissance). No dangerous patterns detected.
valueStatement: Creating browser-based games requires understanding unique platform constraints. This skill provides framework selection guidance, WebGPU adoption strategies, and performance optimization techniques for building high-quality web games.
limitations: Does not provide code execution or project generation capabilities
No direct integration with game engines or build tools
Static guidance only - does not debug or test implementations
Framework-specific code samples are not included

useCases.targetUser: Game developers new to web platform
useCases.title: Choose Framework for 2D Game
useCases.description: A developer starting a 2D browser game needs to select between Phaser (full features) or PixiJS (rendering performance). The decision tree helps identify the right choice based on project requirements.

useCases.title: Enable WebGPU Support
useCases.description: A team building a graphics-intensive game wants to use WebGPU for better performance. The skill provides browser support matrix and fallback strategy for older browsers.

useCases.title: Optimize Game Load Time
useCases.description: A developer notices their web game loads slowly on mobile. The skill guides asset compression (KTX2, Draco, WebP) and lazy loading strategies to reduce initial load.

promptTemplates.title: Framework Selection Help
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to build a [2D/3D] browser game with [specific features]. Which framework should I use?
promptTemplates.scenario: When deciding on a game framework for a web project

promptTemplates.title: WebGPU Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I implement WebGPU with WebGL fallback for my browser game?
promptTemplates.scenario: When modernizing graphics to use WebGPU

promptTemplates.title: PWA Game Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the requirements to make my browser game a Progressive Web App with offline support?
promptTemplates.scenario: When adding installability and offline features

promptTemplates.title: Asset Optimization Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: What compression formats should I use for textures, audio, and 3D models in my web game?
promptTemplates.scenario: When optimizing game assets for web delivery

bestPractices: Start with WebGPU but always provide WebGL fallback for broader browser support (~73% WebGPU coverage)
Compress all assets using KTX2 for textures, WebM/Opus for audio, and glTF with Draco for 3D models
Implement tab visibility handling - pause game loop when browser tab is hidden to prevent resource waste
antiPatterns: Loading all game assets at startup instead of progressive loading
Ignoring audio autoplay restrictions by not requiring user interaction
Assuming all users have fast connections - always handle slow network conditions

faq.question: What is the best framework for 2D browser games?
faq.answer: Phaser 4 is best for full game features. PixiJS 8 is best for rendering-focused projects that need raw performance.

faq.question: Should I use WebGPU or WebGL?
faq.answer: For new projects, use WebGPU with WebGL fallback. This gives access to modern graphics while supporting ~73% of browsers.

faq.question: How do I make my game installable?
faq.answer: Create a web app manifest, add a service worker for caching, and serve over HTTPS. This enables home screen installation and offline play.

faq.question: What asset formats should I use?
faq.answer: Use KTX2 + Basis Universal for textures, WebM/Opus for audio, and glTF with Draco compression for 3D models.

faq.question: Why does audio not play automatically?
faq.answer: Browser autoplay policies require user interaction. Create AudioContext on the first click or tap event.

faq.question: How do I optimize for mobile?
faq.answer: Compress assets aggressively, implement lazy loading, handle touch input properly, and test on low-end devices.
